free_text: Levels of 32S studied by the 31P(p,alpha0)28Si reaction
1-2.8 MeV energy range.
2.8-5.5 MeV energy range.
Four surface barrier detectors with 25 mm2
active area, and 300 microm depletion depth were
used.
The surface barrier detectors were mounted in a 20.3
cm diam scattering chamber. Each detector subtended a
solid angle of approximately 0.0025 sr. Yield curve
measurements were carried out only between 1.98 and
2.10 MeV, where several closely spaced resonances
occur. Energy steps of approximately 1 keV were
taken. Conventional electronics were employed; the
outputs of the solid-state detectors were fed, after
amplification, into a TMC 400-channel analyser. The
charge collected was integrated by means of an Eldorado
Model A309A current integrator; a total charge of 60
microC was collected for each run. Beam currents of
approximately 0.1 microA were used.
BP
Spin and parity assignments are uncertain.
Resonance energies and widths were extracted
directly from the yield curve measurements. The
measured resonance widths were corrected for target
thickness and beam energy spread. The area under the
resonance was taken to be proportional to the product
sigmaxGamma of the resonant cross section (sigma) and
the width (Gamma).
The beam energy spread.
Width error.
Table 1, page 650.
The targets consisted of natural red phosphorus
evaporated onto thin carbon backings. The target
thickness was determined by low energy Rutherford
scattering. Between 1 and 3 MeV, a 6 microg/cm2 target
was used:  for most of the measurements above 3 MeV,
the target thickness was  17 microg/cm2.
For resonance peak cross sections, the relative error
is estimated to be 20 percent, and the absolute error
about 25 percent.
